      <title>C4Media Inc.: Mobile Developer</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state>Ontario</state>
      <skills>TypeScript and React Native</skills>
      <category>Front-End Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description><img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/4826/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" />

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Toronto, Ontario
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://www.c4media.com/">https://www.c4media.com/</a>
</p>

<p>C4Media, Inc. (HQ Toronto, Canada. Private. Established in 2007), is seeking a <strong>Mobile Developer, React Native</strong> (contractor) to join our fully remote, global, fun, and friendly team.</p>
<p>As a Mobile Developer, you will focus on execution and delivery, building and maintaining mobile features end-to-end—from consuming APIs and managing state to rendering polished, production-ready UI on iOS and Android.</p>
<p><strong>The ideal candidate:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>
<p>Is comfortable sitting between frontend and backend concerns</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Can take a spec and turn it into a complete mobile feature</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Understands that most bugs live in data flow and edge cases</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Is pragmatic about architecture and tooling</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Takes responsibility for the stability of what they ship</p>
</li>
</ul>
<h2>What You'll Do</h2>
<ul>
<li>
<p><strong>Implement Mobile Features:</strong> Build complete features in React Native, from API integration to UI rendering.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Data &amp; State Handling:</strong> Consume REST APIs, manage async data flows, caching, and error states.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Cross-Platform Delivery:</strong> Ensure features behave consistently across iOS and Android.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Production Readiness:</strong> Handle loading states, retries, offline scenarios, and edge cases.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Collaboration:</strong> Work with backend and product teams to implement requirements accurately and efficiently.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<ul>
<li><strong>Maintain Codebase:</strong> Improve existing code, refactor when needed, and keep technical debt under control.</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Job Requirements&nbsp;</strong></p>
<p><strong>Professional Experience, Skills &amp; Education:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>
<p>3–5 years of professional mobile development experience</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Strong proficiency in React Native</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Solid TypeScript skills</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Experience integrating APIs (REST)</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Experience with mobile state management (Redux)</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Good understanding of mobile performance considerations</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Familiarity with iOS and Android platform differences</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Experience shipping apps to production</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Portfolio required: Examples of shipped apps, repositories, or concrete feature work</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Only candidates who submit their <strong>applications in English</strong> will be considered for this role (including resume)</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Setup Requirements:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>
<p>Able to work in a full-time remote position</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Reside in Europe</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Able to work as an independent contractor</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Able to work in overlap with our core global office hours (9 AM - 1 PM EST)</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Quiet home office and ability to work comfortably from home</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Reliable infrastructure: Access to high-speed internet and a modern computer</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Able and willing to travel to locations in the USA or Europe 1–3 times per year, with an average stay of 4–8 days, to attend our software conferences and annual company meetings</p>
</li>
</ul>
<h2>Why work for C4Media:</h2>
<ul>
<li>
<p><strong>Work from home - always:</strong> We are a remote-first and remote-always team who has been successfully operating on a work-from-home basis since 2007. And we have no intention of changing that.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Travel the world:</strong> C4Media offers an opportunity to travel 1 - 3 times a year at our expense to NYC, SF, London, and other fun, global locations for conferences &amp; team building. We also got you covered to add a sightseeing day to the end of the trip!&nbsp;</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Take care of each other:</strong> We look out for one another and prioritize respect, fairness, support, and well-being. Check out our core values on our careers page.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Learn something new:</strong> C4Media’s culture is one of learning &amp; mastering. Everyone has a training and education budget for professional growth every year and is encouraged to use it.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Make friends across the world:</strong> Be a part of a leading, fast-growing international company and build a network of international friends and colleagues for life.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Support wellbeing:</strong> In an effort to make physical activity more readily accessible, we offer staff an annual subsidy towards fitness and wellness.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Generous paid time off:</strong> In addition to 25 paid days off in the 1st year and 30 paid days off in every subsequent year, we provide 1 paid day off for birthdays (or a similar special day) and 2 paid days for continuing education.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<div>
<p>&nbsp;</p>
</div>
<div>
<p>&nbsp;</p>
</div>
<div>
<p>&nbsp;</p>
</div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/c4media-inc-mobile-developer">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/c4media-inc-mobile-developer</a></p>
</description>

      <title>Hard Rock Digital: Product Analyst - Back Office</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Front-End Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> ENG, United Kingdom
</p>

<div class="sc-1gch2qa-0 fhKCjg"><div><div class="sc-gfrfnr-2 deOrHQ"></div><div class="sc-18h7are-0 ebVyJP"><div class="sc-1tu9fip-2 ezRMeN"><div class="sc-1tu9fip-1 kdUgoN"><div class="sc-1tu9fip-3 iFQjUO"><div class="sc-ji5wy4-0 jIYAZB"><div><div><div><div><div class="sc-1uwf3m5-2 kxtNOL"><div class="sc-1v95195-0 jscoSJ"><div><div><h2 class="sc-1npqnwg-4 sc-1npqnwg-5 sc-1uwf3m5-0 kdXoTS custom-css-style-job-description">Job description</h2><div class="sc-1fwbcuw-0 OSScx"><div><p class="MsoNormal"><strong>What are we building?&nbsp;</strong></p><p class="MsoNormal">Hard Rock Digital is on a mission to become the premier destination for online sportsbook, gaming, and social gaming. We’re building products for millions of users with integrity, innovation, and a deep respect for the regulatory frameworks that govern our industry.</p><p class="MsoNormal">&nbsp;</p><p class="MsoNormal">Rooted in the legacy of Hard Rock and the Seminole Tribe of Florida, our brand is synonymous with excellence in entertainment, gaming, and hospitality, and we’re bringing that same standard to the digital space. Ready to join a team built for growth and impact?</p><p class="MsoNormal"></p><p class="MsoNormal"><strong>What’s the position?</strong></p><p class="MsoNormal">We are seeking a motivated, detail-oriented Product Analyst to support the development of our internal admin tools and frameworks. In this role, you will work closely with product managers, engineers, and operational stakeholders to improve the tools that power our business behind the scenes.</p><p></p><p class="MsoNormal">This is an excellent opportunity for someone looking to develop strong product fundamentals while contributing to critical internal systems that directly impact daily operations.</p><p class="MsoNormal">&nbsp;</p><p class="MsoNormal"><strong>Key Responsibilities:</strong></p><ul><li><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpFirst"><span>Assist product leaders in developing and executing the product roadmap by supporting research, requirements gathering, documentation, and backlog grooming</span></p></li><li><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle"><span>Help refine and document requirements to support the development of internal tools</span></p></li><li><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle"><span>Gather feedback from teams to understand operational pain points and feature needs</span></p></li><li><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle"><span>Collaborate closely with engineering, design, and QA teams throughout feature development and testing</span></p></li><li><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle"><span>Support product-related questions and issues by working with operations, support, and technical teams to investigate and resolve problems</span></p></li><li><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle"><span>Assist in tracking basic KPIs related to tool performance, adoption, and operational efficiency</span></p></li><li><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle"><span>Support internal stakeholder communication by documenting requirements, updates, and key decisions</span></p></li><li><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle"><span>Apply agile development practices in a fast-paced, iterative environment</span></p></li><li><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpLast"><span>Leverage a startup mindset by taking ownership of your work and driving toward high-quality outcomes</span></p></li></ul></div></div><h2 class="sc-1npqnwg-4 sc-1npqnwg-5 sc-1uwf3m5-0 sc-1uwf3m5-1 cvcKWr custom-css-style-job-requirements">Job requirements</h2><div class="sc-1fwbcuw-0 OSScx"><div><p class="MsoNormal"><strong>What are we looking for?</strong></p><p class="MsoNormal">We are looking for a motivated, analytical problem-solver who is eager to learn and contribute to the development and improvement of internal operational tools. The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, comfortable getting into the weeds, and naturally curious about how systems actually work.</p><p class="MsoNormal">&nbsp;</p><p class="MsoNormal">This is not an engineering role, but you should be comfortable investigating issues independently. This includes opening browser developer tools, examining data flows, reviewing configurations when appropriate, and tracing problems to their root cause rather than stopping at surface-level explanations. You take ownership of problems, ask the right questions, and do not move on until you understand why something behaves the way it does.</p><p class="MsoNormal">&nbsp;</p><p class="MsoNormal">You are driven by improving internal workflows and enabling teams to work more effectively. You bring a hands-on, investigative mindset to every initiative, with a focus on delivering reliable, well-understood solutions that meaningfully support the business.</p><p class="MsoNormal">&nbsp;</p><p class="MsoNormal">The ideal candidate will have:</p><ul><li><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpFirst">A basic understanding of product development processes and methodologies</p></li><li><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle">A strong interest in process automation and workflow optimization</p></li><li><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle">Comfort working with data and internal tools such as dashboards, admin interfaces, spreadsheets, or basic queries</p></li><li><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle">A passion for learning and an eagerness to contribute in a fast-paced environment</p></li><li><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle">Strong written and verbal communication skills for collaborating across teams</p></li><li><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle">Excellent teamwork skills, including the ability to work across multiple time zones</p></li><li><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle">Basic analytical skills to track tool performance and identify areas for improvement</p></li><li><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpLast">A bias toward action, with the ability to move work forward without waiting for perfect direction</p></li></ul><p class="MsoNormal">&nbsp;</p><p class="MsoNormal"><strong>What’s in it for you?</strong></p><p class="MsoNormal">We offer our employees more than just competitive compensation. Our team benefits include:</p><ul><li><p>Competitive pay and benefits</p></li><li><p>Flexible vacation allowance</p></li><li><p>Flexible work from home or office hours</p></li><li><p>Startup culture backed by a secure, global brand&nbsp;</p></li><li><p>Opportunity to build products enjoyed by millions as part of a passionate team</p></li></ul><p class="MsoNormal"><span>&nbsp;</span></p><p class="MsoNormal"><strong>Roster of Uniques</strong></p><p class="MsoNormal">We care deeply about every interaction our customers have with us, and trust and empower our staff to own and drive their experience. Our vision for our business and customers is built on fostering a diverse and inclusive work environment where regardless of background or beliefs you feel able to be authentic and bring all your talent into play. We want to celebrate you being you (we are an equal opportunity employer).</p></div></div></div></div></div><div class="sc-1uwf3m5-3 cTUFI"><div class="sc-e5uvhz-0 kHqIkC"><div class="sc-e5uvhz-8 ekemGm"></div></div></div></div></div></div><div></div><div><div><div class="sc-1f8x0pm-0 ibrjJt"><div><h3 class="sc-1npqnwg-4 sc-1npqnwg-5 sc-1f8x0pm-1 fVrtZz">All done!</h3><p class="sc-1npqnwg-9 sc-1npqnwg-10 EutPU">Your application has been successfully submitted!</p></div><div class="sc-1f8x0pm-2 dvyFlr"><a class="sc-s03za1-0 bwzzvb custom-css-style-application-success-button" href="/">Other jobs</a></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/hard-rock-digital-product-analyst-back-office">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/hard-rock-digital-product-analyst-back-office</a></p>
</description>

      <title>Mmdsmart Ltd: Senior Full Stack Developer (Go/React)</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Front-End Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Poland
</p>

<p>We are seeking a&nbsp;<strong>Senior Full Stack Developer&nbsp;</strong>to join our development team. Our products require innovative and open-minded engineers working in an Agile environment. You will be part of a world-class team of engineers that focuses on new functionality and stabilization for our large-scale system. As a part of your team, you will have the opportunity to learn from others and drive your own initiatives.</p><p>Since 2007, MMDSmart has been a&nbsp;<a href="https://mmdsmart.com/">leading supplier in cloud-based business communications</a>, delivering game-changing IT products to businesses in over 100 countries. Our success is fueled by the inspiration and innovative ideas of our team members, encouraging their growth and impacting our Messaging and&nbsp;<a href="https://mmdsmart.com/retail-voice/">Voice solutions</a>. These empower our clients to communicate more effectively with their customers. Don’t miss this chance to excel in a rapidly expanding tech company, where your potential and ambitions are not just recognized, but celebrated.</p> <p><strong><br>What will you do at MMDSmart?</strong></p><ul><li>Participation in designing and developing new features and projects, occasionally leading to the realization of&nbsp; new development opportunities;</li><li>Presenting demos as part of your team to stakeholders;</li><li>Investigating, diagnosing, and resolving product issues, striving for constant product improvement.</li></ul><p><strong><br>You are a perfect match if you have:</strong></p><ul><li>4+ years of commercial experience as Full Stack Developer;</li><li>3+ years of experience with GoLang;</li><li>2+ years of experience with React.js</li><li>Strong knowledge of TypeScript;</li><li>Experience with SQL and NoSQL databases;</li><li>Ability to work independently and in a team environment.</li></ul> <p><strong>Why do talents choose and stay at MMDSmart?</strong></p><p></p><p>Embrace the opportunity to work in a company that cares about:</p><p></p><p><em>Your Wellness: </em>Access to mental health programs, comprehensive medical insurance, and a sports card*.</p><p><em>Your Happiness: </em>Enjoy the company of cool teammates, a friendly atmosphere, a strong mentorship, and a variety of bonuses &amp; corporate gifts.</p><p><em>Your Development: </em>Benefit from free educational events &amp; courses, and English classes with native speakers.</p><p><em>Your Career:</em> Focus on your professional development in a supportive environment.</p><p><em>Work-Life Balance: </em>Choose your working format - remote, office-based, or hybrid and engage in team-building activities.</p><p></p><p>Think this role's got your name on it? Go ahead and apply, we'd love to see what you've got!</p><p>Equal-Opportunity Employment</p><p><em>We are an equal opportunity employer committed to creating a diverse and inclusive work environment. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.</em></p><p>*Medical Insurance and Sport Card perks are available for employees located in Bulgaria.</p><p></p>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/mmdsmart-ltd-senior-full-stack-developer-go-react">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/mmdsmart-ltd-senior-full-stack-developer-go-react</a></p>
</description>

    <item>
      <title>Brightwheel: Staff AI Product Builder</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Front-End Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> United States
</p>

<p><strong>Our Mission and Opportunity</strong></p><p>Early education is one of the greatest determinants of childhood outcomes, is a must for working families, and has lasting social and economic impact. <a href="https://mybrightwheel.com/careers/">Brightwheel’s vision</a> is high quality early education for every child. We pursue this by directly supporting teachers in the classroom, engaging parents in the development of their kids, and enabling the small businesses that make up the backbone of the $175 billion early education industry. Brightwheel is the most loved technology brand in early education, trusted by tens of thousands of providers and millions of educators and families.</p><p></p><p><strong>Our Team</strong></p><p>Our team is passionate, talented, and customer-focused. We embody our Leadership Principles in our work and culture. We are a distributed team with remote employees across every US time zone, as well as select offices in the US and internationally. Our exceptional investor group includes Addition, Bessemer, Emerson Collective, Lowercase Capital, Mark Cuban, Notable Capital, and others.</p><p></p><p><strong>Who You Are</strong></p><p>You are a Staff level full-stack builder who is both AI-native and product-minded. You love taking an ambiguous customer problem, turning it into a clear plan, and shipping a real end-to-end experience that moves a meaningful outcome. You care about craft and trust in what you ship, and you leave behind reusable building blocks so the next team can move even faster.</p><p></p><p>You will succeed in this role if you are:</p><ul><li><p>Driven by outcomes. You care about helping preschools and childcare centers stay full, save time, and serve families better – not just about shipping “an AI feature.”</p></li><li><p>AI-native. You treat AI as part of your toolchain: you rely on modern AI coding assistants and IDEs and lightweight automation or orchestration tools to move dramatically faster, and you know when AI adds real leverage versus when a simple service or query is enough.</p></li><li><p>A product-driving technical leader. You don’t wait for perfect requirements — you validate hypotheses with prototypes, talk to customers and internal teams, define success metrics, and use working software to align others and drive decisions.</p></li><li><p>Full stack with a platform mindset. You are comfortable designing data models and APIs, implementing backend logic, and building the front-end experiences that sit on top – and you enjoy creating shared services and patterns that other teams can reuse.</p></li><li><p>Thoughtful about AI’s limits. You understand hallucinations, safety, and evaluation, and you design data flows, UX, and guardrails around those constraints rather than ignoring them.</p></li><li><p>Security-minded. You handle sensitive school, educator, and family data with care and instinctively look for secure designs and least-privilege access patterns.</p></li></ul><p></p><p><strong>What You’ll Do</strong></p><p>In this role, you’ll own AI-powered improvements in core brightwheel workflows end-to-end, from opportunity sizing to launch to iteration. You’ll ship experiences that make administrators and teachers faster and more effective, while creating shared patterns that help the rest of engineering build safely and consistently. You will:</p><ul><li><p>Design and build cross-cutting AI services (such as retrieval, context, evaluation, and guardrails) that power multiple product areas like classroom workflows, billing, and family communication.</p></li><li><p>As a hybrid PM+Eng+Data builder: own the end-to-end product loop for the problems you take on: talk to customers and internal teams, define the success metric, design the workflow and user experience, shape the data and evaluation plan, and ship iterative releases from prototype to reliable, scalable production.</p></li><li><p>Create shared abstractions and tooling for AI – for example, common prompt and tool patterns, logging and monitoring, and reusable components – so other engineers can build on a consistent foundation.</p></li><li><p>Shape our data and system architecture so AI can safely stitch together longitudinal signals across product, billing, support, and operations and recommend what should happen next, not just report what happened.</p></li><li><p>Lead by example in AI-augmented engineering, using AI to multiply your own speed, mentoring L2/L3 engineers, and raising the bar for how we design, ship, and operate AI-powered features.</p></li></ul><p></p><p><strong>What You’ve Done</strong></p><p>We are open to a variety of backgrounds, but you likely bring:</p><ul><li><p>5+ years of professional software engineering experience, with clear ownership of medium-to-large production systems from problem statement and design doc through launch and iteration.</p></li><li><p>A proven track record of shipping AI-powered products to production, with concrete examples where LLMs meaningfully improved a metric like engagement, time saved, satisfaction, or retention across one or more product areas.</p></li><li><p>Hands-on experience with large language models (LLMs) in real applications, including prompt and tool design, retrieval-style patterns (such as RAG), and evaluation and monitoring in production.</p></li><li><p>Strong computer science fundamentals (e.g., data structures, algorithms, and systems design) and a generalist mindset, comfortable moving between backend, data, and UX to get the job done.</p></li><li><p>Backend engineering skills in at least one modern web stack (such as Ruby on Rails, Python, Go, or Node), plus confidence with relational databases and larger datasets, from data modeling to performant queries and analytics.</p></li><li><p>Experience building modern web front-ends, ideally with React or a similar component-based framework.</p><p></p></li></ul><p><strong>Nice-to-haves:</strong></p><ul><li><p>Formal training in computer science (4-year CS degree or equivalent depth in core CS topics).</p></li><li><p>A portfolio of personal AI projects, open-source work, or writing that shows how you think about applied AI in real-world settings.</p></li><li><p>Background in vertical SaaS, ecommerce, or other operations-heavy domains.</p></li><li><p>Experience designing shared platforms or frameworks (for example, internal SDKs, evaluation services, or experimentation tooling) adopted by multiple teams.</p></li><li><p>A track record of raising the bar for quality and operations: writing secure, testable, maintainable code; automating and simplifying dev/test/ops workflows; writing and reviewing design docs; mentoring other engineers; and contributing to hiring through interviews and feedback.</p></li></ul><p></p><p><strong>Technology</strong><br>We work with:</p><ul><li><p>Backend: Ruby on Rails, Sidekiq</p></li><li><p>Data: PostgreSQL on Amazon RDS, Redis, and event and analytics pipelines</p></li><li><p>Frontend: React with TypeScript and Emotion</p></li><li><p>Mobile: Native iOS (Swift) and Android (Kotlin with Jetpack Compose)</p></li><li><p>Cloud &amp; Infrastructure: Docker, Kubernetes on Amazon EKS, GitHub Actions and FluxCD for CI/CD, and AWS services such as S3, CloudFront, CloudWatch, and SNS</p></li><li><p>AI &amp; Automation: AWS Bedrock and other hosted large language models, vector search, orchestration and agent frameworks, and modern AI coding tools like Cursor</p></li></ul><p><em>Brightwheel is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive work environment and is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.</em></p><p></p><p><em>Protecting Our Applicants: Please be aware of recruiting scams impersonating Brightwheel. All legitimate communications come from <strong>@</strong></em><a href="http://mybrightwheel.com"><em><strong>mybrightwheel.com</strong></em></a><em> addresses, and we never ask for payment or sensitive personal data as part of our hiring process. If you suspect fraudulent contact, reach out to </em><a href="mailto:security@mybrightwheel.com"><em><strong>security@mybrightwheel.com</strong></em></a><em>. Thank you for helping us keep our applicant community safe.</em></p>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/brightwheel-staff-ai-product-builder">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/brightwheel-staff-ai-product-builder</a></p>
</description>

<p>The mission of Speechify is to make sure that reading is never a barrier to learning.</p><br><p>Over 30 million people use Speechify’s text-to-speech products to turn whatever they’re reading – PDFs, books, Google Docs, news articles, websites – into audio, so they can read faster, read more, and remember more. Speechify’s text-to-speech reading products include its iOS app, Android App, Mac App, Chrome Extension, and Web App. Google recently named Speechify the Chrome Extension of the Year and Apple named Speechify its App of the Day.</p><br><p>Today, nearly 200 people around the globe work on Speechify in a 100% distributed setting – Speechify has no office. These include frontend and backend engineers, AI research scientists, and others from Amazon, Microsoft, and Google, leading PhD programs like Stanford, high growth startups like Stripe, Vercel, Bolt, and many founders of their own companies.</p><br><p>&nbsp;This is a key role and ideal for someone who thinks strategically, enjoys fast-paced environments, passionate about making product decisions, and has experience building great user experiences that delight users.</p><br><p>We are a flat organization that allows anyone to become a leader by showing excellent technical skills and delivering results consistently and fast. Work ethic, solid communication skills, and obsession with winning are paramount.&nbsp;</p><br><p>Our interview process involves several technical interviews and we aim to complete them within 1 week.&nbsp;</p><br><p><strong>What Yo</strong><strong>u’</strong><strong>ll Do</strong></p><br><ul><br><li>Actively ship production code to the web products</li><br><li>Work closely with your dedicated product team</li><br><li>Participate in product discussions to shape the product roadmap</li><br><li>Have the opportunity to work on new and exciting features that will impact millions of lives</li><br></ul><br><p><strong>An Ideal Candidate Should Have</strong></p><br><ul><br><li>Experience. You've built and ship products that have scaled to thousands or millions of users</li><br><li>Customer obsession. You are passionate about the field and have the desire to build high quality product that serves customer needs</li><br><li>Speed. You work quickly to generate ideas and know how to decide which things can ship now and what things need time</li><br><li>Focus. We’re a high-growth startup with a busy, remote team. You know how and when to engage or be heads down</li><br><li>Collaboration. You know how to inspire, play, and negotiate with opinionated designers, marketers, and PMs</li><br><li>Tech Stack: Vanilla JS, ReactJS, Redux, Firebase, Typescript</li><br></ul><br><p><strong>What We Offer </strong></p><br><ul><br><li>A fast-growing environment where you can help shape the culture</li><br><li>An entrepreneurial crew that supports risk, intuition, and hustle</li><br><li>A hands-off approach so you can focus and do your best work</li><br><li>The opportunity to make an impact in a transformative industry</li><br><li>A competitive salary, a collegiate atmosphere, and a commitment to building a great asynchronous culture</li><br></ul><br><p><strong>Compensation:</strong> The US base salary range for this full-time position is $140,000-$200,000 + bonus + equity depending on experience</p><br><p><strong>Think you’re a good fit for this job?&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;&nbsp;</strong></p><br><p>Tell us more about yourself and why you're interested in the role when you apply. <br>And don’t forget to include links to your portfolio and LinkedIn.</p><br><p><strong>Not looking but know someone who would make a great fit?&nbsp;</strong></p><br><p>Refer them!&nbsp;</p><br><p><strong>Speechify is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace.&nbsp;</strong></p><br><p>Speechify does not discriminate on the basis of race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, or other legally protected status.</p>

      <title>EventMobi: Front End Engineer - Remote</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state>Ontario</state>
      <skills>HTML/CSS, React, Testing/Debugging, Front-End Dev, and TypeScript</skills>
      <category>Front-End Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description><img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0064/4101/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" />

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> 151 Yonge Street 11th Floor Toronto, Ontario M5C 2W7
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://www.eventmobi.com/">https://www.eventmobi.com/</a>
</p>

<p><em>*** EventMobi is an all-remote company and this is a fully remote position, open to all candidates with a focus in Pakistan, India, Brazil, Columbia, Argentina and Mexico.&nbsp;</em></p>
<p><em>You will need to be able to work in EST time zones. ***</em></p>
<p>&nbsp;</p>
<p><strong>WHY YOU SHOULD JOIN US?</strong></p>
<p>At EventMobi, you won’t just build UI components. &nbsp;You’ll help deliver meaningful product experiences used by thousands of organizations around the world.</p>
<p>Our front-end applications are large-scale React/TypeScript systems with real-world complexity: offline capabilities, cross-device support, and performance-sensitive rendering. You’ll work on features that directly impact users, while developing a deeper understanding of how modern front-end systems are designed and evolve over time.</p>
<p>This role is ideal for engineers who are strong in fundamentals, take pride in their craft, and want to grow into more advanced system design and ownership over time.<br>We believe great engineers take ownership of their work, collaborate openly, and continuously improve. You’ll be supported by experienced teammates while having the autonomy to contribute meaningfully from day one.</p>
<p>We also believe the next generation of engineers will be AI-native. You’ll be encouraged to use AI-assisted tools to improve productivity, code quality, and learning speed.</p>
<p><strong>HEAR FROM OUR FRONT-END ENGINEER, MOEED:&nbsp;</strong></p>
<p><strong><em>“I’ve been here a little over 2 years, working across the front end, mostly focused on onsite and registration flows. What I have enjoyed the most here is a mix of real ownership and strong support. &nbsp;You’re trusted to lead meaningful work, but there’s always someone ready to jump in and debug or brainstorm with you.&nbsp;</em></strong></p>
<p><strong><em>There’s also never a shortage of genuinely interesting technical challenges, which keep things fun and help you grow. The team is very collaborative and product‑minded, and being here has pushed me to think beyond ‘just code’ to how what I build actually impacts customers and the business.”</em></strong></p>
<p><strong>WHAT YOUR WORK WILL FOCUS ON:</strong></p>
<p>This role sits at the intersection of hands-on development, product thinking, and continuous growth.</p>
<p>Build Modern Web Experiences</p>
<ul>
<li>Develop responsive, high-quality interfaces using React &amp; JavaScript/TypeScript</li>
<li>Contribute to features within our Progressive Web App</li>
<li>Help deliver reliable experiences across devices and varying network conditions</li>
</ul>
<p>Contribute to Feature Development</p>
<ul>
<li>Implement features from defined requirements and collaborate on design decisions</li>
<li>Work closely with product managers, designers, and senior engineers</li>
<li>Balance user experience, performance, and technical constraints</li>
</ul>
<p>Work Within Scalable Front-End Systems</p>
<ul>
<li>Contribute to a shared codebase with established patterns and practices</li>
<li>Participate in discussions around architecture and system improvements</li>
<li>Learn how to navigate and improve large, evolving applications</li>
</ul>
<p>Collaborate and Improve as a Team</p>
<ul>
<li>Participate in code reviews and technical discussions</li>
<li>Apply feedback to continuously improve code quality and approach</li>
<li>Contribute to a culture of collaboration, clarity, and shared ownership</li>
</ul>
<p>Work Smarter with AI</p>
<ul>
<li>Use AI-assisted tools (e.g. Claude, Codex) to accelerate development</li>
<li>Improve productivity in debugging, testing, and implementation tasks</li>
<li>Continuously refine how you integrate AI into your workflow</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>YOUR QUALIFICATIONS:</strong></p>
<p>You’re a solid engineer with strong fundamentals and a desire to grow.</p>
<ul>
<li>3–5 years of experience building user-facing web applications</li>
<li>2+ years of experience with JavaScript/TypeScript and React</li>
<li>Strong understanding of modern HTML, CSS, and front-end fundamentals</li>
<li>Experience working with APIs and integrating backend services</li>
<li>Ability to debug issues and work within existing codebases</li>
<li>Familiarity with testing (unit and end-to-end)</li>
<li>Clear written communication skills (we’re async-first)</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>NICE TO HAVE:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Experience with state management or data-fetching libraries (e.g., React Query)</li>
<li>Exposure to performance optimization or scalable front-end patterns</li>
<li>Familiarity with CI/CD workflows or cloud environments</li>
<li>Interest in mobile or cross-platform development</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>THIS ROLE IS A GREAT FIT FOR YOU IF..</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>You enjoy building real features that users rely on</li>
<li>You’re comfortable working with some ambiguity but appreciate guidance</li>
<li>You want to grow your skills in architecture and system design over time</li>
<li>You value feedback and continuous improvement</li>
<li>You’re excited about using modern tools, including AI, to work more effectively</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>THIS ROLE IS PROBABLY <em>NOT&nbsp;</em>FOR YOU IF..</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>You’re primarily looking to define architecture and lead technical direction</li>
<li>You prefer highly structured, narrowly scoped tasks with little ambiguity</li>
<li>You’re not interested in collaborating closely or receiving feedback</li>
<li>You’re uncomfortable working in a remote, async-first environment</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>OUR INTERVIEW PROCESS</strong></p>
<p>If your profile moves forward, here’s what you can expect during the hiring process:</p>
<ol>
<li>Introductory Conversation (45 minutes)<br>Meet with the VP of Product &amp; Engineering to discuss your background, experience, and career goals.</li>
<li>Technical Assessment (120 minutes)<br>Participate in a live, hands-on session with members of our technical team to demonstrate your problem-solving and coding skills.</li>
<li>Project Deep Dive (90 minutes)<br>Walk us through impactful projects you’ve worked on, highlighting your contributions, decisions, and outcomes.</li>
<li>Offer Stage<br>Final discussions and next steps.<br><br></li>
</ol>
<p>We’ll provide more details at each stage and ensure you have plenty of opportunities to ask questions along the way.</p>
<p><strong>BONUS</strong></p>
<p>Hey - If you’ve made it this far, are excited about what you’ve read and feel that you have what it takes, then we’d love to hear from you.</p>
<p>Please use this link to submit a <a href="https://app.hireflix.com/public-application/69a7158e8de78d13d3e3c156">short video answering</a>&nbsp;a few questions. We guarantee you’ll receive a response from us within five business days, either way.</p>
<p>And don’t forget to submit your application for the role as well!</p>
<p><strong>WHAT WILL YOU GET FROM EVENTMOBI?&nbsp;</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Compensation: Base salary ranges from $40k to $65k CAD per annual, depending on experience</li>
<li>Time-Off:&nbsp;2-weeks PTO + 3-summer days + your birthday is a &nbsp;holiday (seriously! log-off and hang out with the fam or go do something fun) + opportunities to meet your co-workers in person throughout the year</li>
<li>Tools:&nbsp;Top of the line hardware and the best collaboration software for remote work so you can be as productive as possible while working from home</li>
<li>The Best Coworkers Ever:&nbsp;If you ask anyone that has worked at EventMobi, they will tell you that their fellow Mobiers are the best part of their experience! Don't underestimate the positive impact of caring co-workers have on your everyday happiness.</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>REMOTE WORKSPACE REQUIREMENTS:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>You have a safe, comfortable, and ergonomic workspace and working environment - a private and quiet workspace is a vital requirement for doing your best work!</li>
<li>You have a reliable internet connection capable of participating in video calls, with at least 30Mbps download speed.</li>
<li>Ability to collaborate during EST working hours.</li>
</ul>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/eventmobi-front-end-engineer-remote">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/eventmobi-front-end-engineer-remote</a></p>
</description>

<h2 class="text-3xl font-bold text-primary-dark mb-6 flex items-center">Job Overview</h2>
<p class="text-lg text-neutral-graphite leading-relaxed">We’re hiring a mid-level React Frontend Developer to build smooth, accessible, and performant web interfaces for our products. You will collaborate closely with design and back-end teams, ship quality features, and keep UI standards high with thoughtful testing and reviews.</p>
<p><strong>Responsibilities:</strong></p>
<p>&nbsp; &nbsp; - Build and maintain user-facing features in React + TypeScript<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; - Translate Figma/design specs into responsive, accessible interfaces<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; - Integrate REST/GraphQL APIs and handle client-side state effectively<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; - Write unit/integration tests and participate in code reviews<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; - Measure and improve performance (bundle size, TTI, Core Web Vitals)<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; - Contribute to component libraries, documentation, and UI consistency</p>
<p><strong>Requirements:</strong></p>
<p>&nbsp; &nbsp; - 2–4 years building production React apps with TypeScript<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; - Strong in modern React (hooks), routing, and state management (Context/Redux/Zustand)<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; - Excellent HTML/CSS fundamentals; experience with responsive layouts and accessibility (ARIA, WCAG)<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; - Familiar with testing tools (Jest, React Testing Library) and Git workflows<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; - Comfortable integrating with APIs and handling auth, errors, and loading states<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; - Clear communication, ownership mindset, and collaborative approach</p>
<p><strong>Benefits &amp; Perks:</strong></p>
<p>&nbsp; &nbsp; - Competitive salary with performance bonus potential<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; - Medical, dental, and vision insurance<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; - 401(k) with company match<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; - Generous PTO plus sick leave and holidays<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; - Remote-first with flexible hours<br>&nbsp; &nbsp; - Annual learning stipend and conference support</p>

    <item>
      <title>EventMobi: Senior Front End Engineer - Remote</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state>Ontario</state>
      <skills>JavaScript, React, and Front-End Dev</skills>
      <category>Front-End Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> 151 Yonge Street 11th Floor Toronto, Ontario M5C 2W7
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://www.eventmobi.com/">https://www.eventmobi.com/</a>
</p>

<p><em>*** EventMobi is an all-remote company and this is a fully remote position, open to all candidates with a focus in Pakistan, India, Brazil, Columbia, Argentina and Mexico.&nbsp;</em></p>
<p><em>You will need to be able to work in EST time zones. ***</em></p>
<p>&nbsp;</p>
<p>WHY YOU SHOULD JOIN US?</p>
<p>At EventMobi, you won’t just ship UI features. You’ll help shape the core front-end architecture behind a global event platform used by thousands of organizations that rely on it to perform, scale and evolve.</p>
<p>Our web applications are not thin presentation layers. They are large, offline-capable, cross-device React/TypeScript systems with shared design foundations, complex state management, performance-sensitive rendering and long-term architectural considerations. &nbsp;The decisions you make here compound.</p>
<p>We believe great software is built by engineers who take ownership beyond the ticket. You’ll have the space to challenge assumptions, improve abstractions, influence technical direction, and think in systems - not just components.</p>
<p>We also believe the strongest engineers of the future know how to work with AI. &nbsp;We actively encourage AI-native development to reduce repetitive work, increase quality and ultimately drive higher throughput — so your time goes toward design thinking, architecture, and meaningful problem-solving.</p>
<p>If you care about product thinking, craftsmanship and building software that lasts, you’ll find real leverage here.<br><br></p>
<p>HEAR FROM OUR SENIOR ENGINEER &amp; DEV LEAD ALI:</p>
<p><em>“I joined the team as a Software Engineer five years ago, and what keeps me here is the rare balance between high-level technical challenge and a genuinely sustainable pace. In many senior roles, you expect to spend your days 'extinguishing fires,' but here, the focus is on architectural integrity and long-term stability.</em></p>
<p><em>The engineering culture is built on mutual respect and deep technical expertise. We don't just ship features to hit arbitrary deadlines; we take the time to conduct thorough, meaningful PR reviews that serve as a mentorship tool for the whole team. Having been here for half a decade, I've seen our systems evolve, and I've always felt empowered to take ownership of the roadmap and advocate for the right technical decisions.</em></p>
<p><em>What stands out most is the lack of ego. Even as a Senior, I'm constantly learning from my peers. The environment is collaborative which makes it easy to focus on what actually matters - writing clean, scalable code and solving complex problems.</em></p>
<p><em>If you're a seasoned engineer looking for a place where your experience is valued, your growth is supported, and your work-life balance is actually protected, this is the place to be.”</em></p>
<p><strong>WHAT YOUR WORK WILL FOCUS ON:</strong></p>
<p>This isn’t a “pixel pusher” role. You’ll operate at the intersection of product thinking, architecture, and hands-on engineering.</p>
<p>Build Modern Web Experiences</p>
<ul>
<li>Evolve our high-performance, responsive Progressive Web App</li>
<li>Implement offline-first capabilities, real-time updates, and scalable front-end architecture</li>
<li>Deliver experiences that work flawlessly across devices, even in low-connectivity environments</li>
</ul>
<p>Own Features End-to-End</p>
<ul>
<li>From ideation and design review to implementation, testing, CI/CD, deployment, and monitoring</li>
<li>Implement offline-first capabilities, real-time updates, and scalable front-end architecture</li>
<li>Deliver experiences that work flawlessly across devices, even in low-connectivity environments</li>
</ul>
<p>Architect for Scale</p>
<ul>
<li>Design abstractions that survive real-world complexity</li>
<li>Navigate and improve large codebases and legacy systems</li>
<li>Keep performance, maintainability, and developer experience in balance</li>
</ul>
<p>Raise the Bar</p>
<ul>
<li>Mentor engineers and lead by example</li>
<li>Advocate for clean code, strong testing, and disciplined engineering practices</li>
<li>Contribute to a culture of technical excellence</li>
</ul>
<p>Work Smarter with AI</p>
<ul>
<li>Embrace AI-assisted development using tools like Claude, Codex, and other LLMs to move faster on feature, tech and routine work</li>
<li>Reinvest that time into more ambitious features, stronger code quality, and better long-term systems</li>
<li>Focus on solving high-impact problems instead of repetitive tasks</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>YOUR QUALIFICATIONS:</strong></p>
<p>You’re not just experienced—you’re thoughtful about how software should be built.</p>
<ul>
<li>8+ years building scalable, user-facing software</li>
<li>4+ years of deep experience with JavaScript/TypeScript and React</li>
<li>Strong architectural thinking and problem-solving skills</li>
<li>Comfort working in large, evolving codebases</li>
<li>Experience with REST APIs, JSX, modern HTML/CSS</li>
<li>Automated testing experience (Jest, React Testing Library, end-to-end)</li>
<li>Clear, professional written communication (we’re async-first)</li>
<li>Strong understanding of SOLID principles, domain modeling, and design patterns</li>
<li>A mentorship mindset—you elevate the people around you</li>
</ul>
<p>NICE TO HAVE:</p>
<ul>
<li>Strongly typed TypeScript across hooks and components</li>
<li>React Query or similar API integration libraries</li>
<li>Mobile app development experience (Capacitor, Cordova, React Native)</li>
<li>Docker, AWS, GitHub Actions, and CI/CD pipelines</li>
</ul>
<p>THIS ROLE IS PROBABLY <strong><em>NOT&nbsp;</em></strong>FOR YOU IF..</p>
<p>Let’s be honest. You’ll struggle here if:</p>
<ul>
<li>You prefer tightly scoped, ambiguity-free tasks</li>
<li>You need frequent synchronous meetings to move work forward</li>
<li>You’re uncomfortable making independent decisions</li>
<li>You’re skeptical of AI-assisted workflows</li>
<li>You haven’t worked in a fully remote, async-first environment</li>
</ul>
<p>We value autonomy, clarity in writing, and momentum.</p>
<p><strong>OUR INTERVIEW PROCESS</strong></p>
<p>If your profile moves forward, here’s what you can expect during the hiring process:</p>
<ol>
<li>Introductory Conversation (45 minutes)<br>Meet with the VP of Product &amp; Engineering to discuss your background, experience, and career goals.</li>
<li>Technical Assessment (120 minutes)<br>Participate in a live, hands-on session with members of our technical team to demonstrate your problem-solving and coding skills.</li>
<li>Project Deep Dive (90 minutes)<br>Walk us through impactful projects you’ve worked on, highlighting your contributions, decisions, and outcomes.</li>
<li>Offer Stage<br>Final discussions and next steps.</li>
</ol>
<p>We’ll provide more details at each stage and ensure you have plenty of opportunities to ask questions along the way.</p>
<p>BONUS</p>
<p>Hey - If you’ve made it this far, are excited about what you’ve read and feel that you have what it takes, then we’d love to hear from you.</p>
<p>Please use this link to submit a&nbsp;<a href="https://app.hireflix.com/public-application/69a7158e8de78d13d3e3c156">short video answering&nbsp;</a>a few questions. We guarantee you’ll receive a response from us within five business days, either way.</p>
<p>And don’t forget to submit your application for the role as well!</p>
<p><strong>WHAT WILL YOU GET FROM EVENTMOBI?</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Compensation: Base salary ranges from $50k to $85k CAD per annual, depending on experience</li>
<li>Time-Off:&nbsp;2-weeks PTO + 3-summer days + your birthday is a &nbsp;holiday (seriously! log-off and hang out with the fam or go do something fun) + opportunities to meet your co-workers in person throughout the year</li>
<li>Tools:&nbsp;Top of the line hardware and the best collaboration software for remote work so you can be as productive as possible while working from home</li>
<li>The Best Coworkers Ever:&nbsp;If you ask anyone that has worked at EventMobi, they will tell you that their fellow Mobiers are the best part of their experience! Don't underestimate the positive impact of caring co-workers have on your everyday happiness.</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>REMOTE WORKSPACE REQUIREMENTS:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>You have a safe, comfortable, and ergonomic workspace and working environment - a private and quiet workspace is a vital requirement for doing your best work!</li>
<li>You have a reliable internet connection capable of participating in video calls, with at least 30Mbps download speed</li>
<li>Ability to collaborate during EST working hours</li>
</ul>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/eventmobi-senior-front-end-engineer-remote-1">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/eventmobi-senior-front-end-engineer-remote-1</a></p>
</description>

      <title>Sanctuary Computer: Senior Frontend Developer</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state>New York</state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Front-End Programming</category>
      <type>Contract</type>
      <description><img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/3386/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" />

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> New York City
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://sanctuary.computer">https://sanctuary.computer</a>
</p>

<p><span class="notion-enable-hover">We are hiring a </span><span class="notion-enable-hover">contract-based</span><span class="notion-enable-hover"> <strong>Senior Frontend Developer</strong> for the garden3d creative collective with a <strong>focus on West Coast candidates.&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;<a href="https://garden3d.notion.site/Senior-Frontend-Developer-West-Coast-330131fea2c780c0b713ec7cb882386c?pvs=74">Original job posting link</a></strong></span></p>
<h2><strong>About garden3d</strong></h2>
<p>We are worker owned creative collective, innovating on everything from brands and IRL communities to IoT devices and cross platform apps. We share profit, open source everything, spin out new businesses, and invest in exciting ideas through financial and/or in-kind contributions.</p>
<p>Our client roster includes <a href="https://www.google.com/">Google</a>, <a href="https://stripe.com/">Stripe</a>, <a href="https://www.figma.com/">Figma</a>, <a href="https://hinge.co/">Hinge</a>, <a href="https://blacksocialists.us/">Black Socialists in America</a>, <a href="https://www.aclu.org/">ACLU</a>, <a href="https://www.pratt.edu/">Pratt</a>, <a href="https://www.newschool.edu/parsons/">Parsons</a>, <a href="https://www.mozilla.org/en-US/">Mozilla</a>, <a href="https://www.nobelprize.org/">The Nobel Prize</a>, <a href="https://www.mit.edu/">MIT</a>, <a href="https://www.gnosis.io/">Gnosis</a>, <a href="https://www.etsy.com/">Etsy</a> &amp; <a href="https://gagosian.com/">Gagosian</a>.</p>
<p>We’re the software team behind innovative products like <a href="https://www.thelightphone.com/">The Light Phone</a> &amp; <a href="https://www.mill.com/">Mill</a>, and we operate a global, decentralized community space collective called <a href="https://www.index-space.org/">Index Space</a>.</p>
<p>We think of our garden3d as collective for creative people, prioritizing a happy, talented, and diverse studio culture. We work on projects that bring value to our world, and we balance deep care for the work we do with a genuine curiosity about life outside of our jobs.</p>
<h2><strong>Who we’re looking for:</strong></h2>
<p>We're looking for a <strong>Senior Frontend Developer</strong> who excels at building pixel-perfect websites using modern frontend frameworks. You'll collaborate with our team to build elegant, performant, and visually stunning web experiences. Your work will span a diverse range of client projects, from immersive brand websites to complex web applications, all requiring a keen eye for detail and technical excellence.</p>
<p><strong><span class="notion-enable-hover">This hiring effort will focus on Front-End Developers based in the U.S. West Coast. Candidates in the Pacific Time zone are preferred (LA/SF proximity is a plus). We are also open to candidates located in the Mountain Time zone.</span></strong></p>
<p>In this role, you’ll work on a variety of client projects to find cost-effective, high-quality, pragmatic solutions to complex problems. Responsibilities will include:</p>
<ul>
<li>Collaborating with Technical Lead to meet clients' development needs</li>
<li>Building and maintaining high-performance web applications with modern frontend frameworks and tools</li>
<li>Implementing responsive, accessible, and pixel-perfect user interfaces based on design specifications</li>
<li>Integrating frontend applications with headless CMS platforms, APIs, and third-party services</li>
<li>Optimizing application performance, including bundle size, load times, and runtime efficiency</li>
<li>Architecting scalable component libraries and design systems for consistency across projects</li>
<li>Writing clear documentation for code maintenance and usage</li>
<li>Participating in project team meetings, including Sprint Planning, daily standups, and retrospectives</li>
<li>Participating in code reviews, providing constructive feedback to teammates and ensuring adherence to best practices</li>
</ul>
<p>The person we’re looking for is happy, relaxed and easy to get along with. They’re flexible on anything except conceits that will lower their usually outstanding work quality. They work “smart”, by carefully managing their workflow and staggering features that have dependencies intelligently — they prefer deep work but are OK coming up to the surface now and then for top level / strategic conversations.</p>
<p>We believe people with backgrounds or interests in design, art, music, food or fashion tend to have a well rounded sense of design &amp; quality — so a variety of hobbies or side projects is a big nice to have!</p>
<p><strong>Must Have Competencies:</strong></p>
<p>We’re always pitching for new and exciting technology niches. Some of the areas below are relevant to us!</p>
<ul>
<li>8+ years writing highly performant frontend code, an obsession for 95+ Lighthouse scores</li>
<li>Expert level experience with Typescript, and one of Next.js, Nuxt, Svelte, Vue</li>
<li>Extensive experience with headless CMS like Sanity, Contentful, Prismic or more</li>
<li>Fluency in industry standard PaaS like Vercel, Netlify, Firebase, etc</li>
<li>Fluency in eCommerce technologies like Shopify (headless &amp; liquid), Stripe, Swell and others</li>
<li>Experience building accessible, responsive interfaces with attention to performance optimization and SEO best practices</li>
<li>Strong understanding of modern CSS methodologies (Tailwind, CSS Modules, etc) and animation libraries</li>
<li>Experience with state management solutions (Redux, Zustand, Pinia) and API integration patterns</li>
<li>Proficiency with testing frameworks (Jest, Playwright, Cypress) and commitment to writing maintainable, well-documented code</li>
<li>Experience with design systems and component libraries, working closely with designers to ensure pixel-perfect implementations</li>
<li><strong>Real-time &amp; performance optimization</strong>: experience with WebSockets for live data updates, caching strategies (Redis, CDN-level caching), CDN configuration and optimization (Cloudflare, Fastly), and image optimization techniques including proxies and delivery networks</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Nice to Have Competencies:</strong></p>
<p>We’re always pitching for new and exciting technology niches. Some of the areas below are relevant to us!</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>WebGL &amp; Canvas expertise</strong>: experience building interactive graphics, animations, and visualizations using WebGL, Three.js, or native Canvas API</li>
<li><strong>Data visualization</strong>: creating compelling, interactive data visualizations with libraries like Mapbox, D3.js, Chart.js, or similar tools</li>
<li><strong>Full-stack development experience</strong>: comfortable working across the entire stack, from frontend to backend and database layers</li>
<li><strong>PostgreSQL expertise</strong>: strong experience with database design, query optimization, and managing complex relational data structures</li>
<li><strong>GraphQL &amp; API design</strong>: building and maintaining GraphQL or REST APIs with a focus on performance and developer experience</li>
<li><strong>Real-time technologies</strong>: experience with WebSockets, Server-Sent Events, or similar technologies for building live, interactive features</li>
<li><strong>Authentication &amp; security</strong>: implementing secure authentication flows (OAuth, JWT) and following security best practices</li>
<li><strong>Client-facing experience</strong>: working directly with customers to gather requirements and provide technical solutions</li>
<li><strong>Product management experience</strong>: defining product roadmaps and collaborating closely with stakeholders</li>
<li><strong>Engineering management experience</strong>: leading teams, setting technical direction, and mentoring developers</li>
</ul>
<h2><strong>Compensation</strong></h2>
<p>Our pay scale ranges from <code>$100/hr</code> to <code>$130/hr</code> pending seniority (&amp; team leadership experience), and our projects are rarely less than 8 full time weeks at 40 hours per week.</p>
<p>We prefer long standing relationships with highly accountable and communicative team members, so we encourage candidates to expect longer term engagements.</p>
<h2><strong>How we interview:</strong></h2>
<p>Our interview process starts with a call where you get to meet a few members of our team. From there we’ll ask appropriate candidates to take part in a technical exercise which helps illustrate skill level and comfort.</p>
<h2>‍ <strong>How we work:</strong></h2>
<p>We believe that there’s a better balance between the poles of freelancing &amp; full time, and for that reason Sanctuary works differently to most shops:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="https://sanctucompu.substack.com/p/our-open-source-2020-profit-and-loss"><strong>Transparency &amp; Ownership:</strong></a>&nbsp;We release out Profit &amp; Loss statements to the community each year, open source our best ideas, and talk business &amp; money with everyone in the company. We’re proud to run our business with integrity, and for that reason we share everything with our team &amp; community.</li>
<li><a href="https://negative.sanctuary.computer/">**150% Carbon Negative:</a>** Our studio offsets 150% of the carbon we use to do business each year, dated back to our founding in 2015. We turn down work that is not in-line with our morals, and we encourage our peers to do the same. <a href="https://twitter.com/sanctucompu/status/1385282161197060100">We have been certified climate neutral since 2021</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://sanctucompu.substack.com/p/our-moral-compass">**Strong Morals:</a>** Since our founding, we've turned down somewhere between $1mm - $2mm of work that didn't meet our moral standards. (Most of that was DTC brands that can't show a valid sustainability initiative).</li>
<li><strong>Async &amp; Decentralized:</strong> We use tools optimized for calm, thoughtful communication, and opt for async whenever possible. We fight hard to maintain our focus time.</li>
<li><strong>Remote Friendly:</strong>&nbsp;Our company is fluent in remote work, making our workplace more decentralized, and democratized in the process.</li>
<li><strong>Ideas &amp; Products:</strong>&nbsp;In our spare studio time, we work to build our own open source or internal products to diversify &amp; bolster our income. We create amazing technology products for our clients, so why not for the studio?</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Important Reminders</strong><br><br><strong>Kindly submit a complete and thoughtful application, including relevant links that help verify your work experience and identity. Applications with missing or insufficient information will not move forward in the review process.</strong></p>
<p><strong>Our team carefully reviews every complete submission, and we truly appreciate the time and effort you put into applying.</strong></p>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/sanctuary-computer-senior-frontend-developer-1">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/sanctuary-computer-senior-frontend-developer-1</a></p>
</description>

      <title>FINN: Lead Frontend Engineer</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state>Tbilisi</state>
      <skills>AngularJS, Firebase, TypeScript, and Mobile Development</skills>
      <category>Front-End Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description><img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/4556/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" />

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Singapore
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://www.finn-app.com/">https://www.finn-app.com/</a>
</p>

<p class="p1"><strong>Are you ready to shape the financial future of a rapidly growing fintech that’s transforming lives across Southeast Asia and beyond?</strong></p>
<p class="p1">We’re not your typical company - we’re a rocketship! At <strong>FINN</strong>, established in 2022, by experienced serial founders - we’re on a mission to transform financial well-being for underbanked employees. Our product provides early wage access, financial education, budgeting tools, part-time job opportunities, and micro-insurances. We've hit product-market fit with explosive growth during the past 12 months.</p>
<p class="p1">We need a true <strong>Frontend Lead </strong>who stays deep in the code but also guides the frontend team (currently 2–3 devs, growing).</p>
<p class="p1">This is a <strong>heavily hands-on role </strong>(80–90% coding) with light people leadership (1:1s, code-review ownership, tech direction, mentoring). If you enjoy both building pixel-perfect features yourself and helping others level up, this is for you. Future growth into Head of Frontend is realistic for the right person.</p>
<p class="p1"><strong>What You’ll Own</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Own the entire frontend codebase (Angular 17+, TypeScript, Ionic/Capacitor, Firebase)</li>
<li>Ship our native Android app via Capacitor (and add iOS down the road)</li>
<li>Turn Figma designs into responsive, performant, pixel-perfect UIs</li>
<li>Architect reusable components and maintain a clean, scalable codebase</li>
<li>Drive performance optimization (rendering, lazy loading, bundle size, API efficiency)</li>
<li>Set up and maintain testing strategy (unit with Jest, e2e with Playwright/Cypress)</li>
<li>Run feature-flag and A/B testing flows</li>
<li>Lead code reviews and enforce best practices</li>
<li>Run weekly 1:1s, help unblock the team, and keep frontend velocity high</li>
<li>Collaborate directly with backend, product, and design on a daily basis</li>
<li>Keep us up to date with modern Angular/Ionic ecosystem practices</li>
</ul>
<p class="p1"><strong>What You Bring</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>6+ years frontend development, at least 3–4 years deep with modern Angular working on high-traffic consumer platforms.</li>
<li>Experience in cross-platform frameworks - ideally Capacitor/Ionic.</li>

<p class="p1"><strong>Nice-to-Have</strong></p>

<p class="p1"><strong>Why You’ll Love It Here</strong></p>

<li>Fully remote, flexible hours (as long as you have good overlap with Asia timezones)</li>
<li>Small, high-caliber team that values clean code and shipping fast</li>
</ul>
<p>If you live and breathe Angular/Ionic, care deeply about product and UX, and want to lead while staying hands-on, let’s talk.</p>

      <title>Knack: Senior Frontend Engineer (React / Next.js) — Full-Time, Contract</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state>North Carolina</state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Front-End Programming</category>
      <type>Contract</type>
      <description><img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0000/0069/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" />

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> US
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://knack.com">https://knack.com</a>
</p>

<p><strong>About Knack</strong></p>
<p><a href="https://www.knack.com/">Knack</a>&nbsp;is an AI-powered no-code software development platform enabling organizations to manage, analyze, and share business data. The no-code market is growing at over 30% annually and projected to be almost $200B by 2030. Founded in 2012, Knack has grown into a leading player in the no-code movement, enabling businesses, teams, and individuals to build custom applications without writing a single line of code. Serving a wide range of industries, from healthcare and education to finance and nonprofits, and organizations from small businesses to Fortune 500s, Knack enables users to boost data visibility and streamline operations. To date, the platform has powered 400,000 apps and stored more than 5 billion data records for over 15,000 customers. Its enterprise-grade functionality includes an AI-powered visual builder, robust data structures, user access controls, and automation capabilities, all delivered by a fully remote team committed to trust, continuous improvement, and measurable impact.</p>
<p><strong>The role:</strong></p>
<p>We’re hiring a brilliant Senior Front-end Engineer to help us meet the enormous challenges of building a world-class UI/UX for our new platform, built with React and Asterisk, our custom design system.</p>
<p>We’re looking for someone who is not just a fast Typescript coder, but also&nbsp;<strong>cares deeply about our customers' experiences</strong>, code quality, and architecture, can switch contexts easily, and enjoys diving into the deep end of challenging work.</p>
<p>We seek applicants who are passionate about what matters to them and want to join a fun, close-knit team of high-quality individuals who, above all, enjoy working together!</p>
<p><strong>In this role, you will:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Leverage React and Typescript&nbsp;</strong>to craft clean, beautiful, maintainable, extensible, and secure code.</li>
<li>Refactor and modernize our codebases to increase maintainability, performance, and rapid iteration.</li>
<li>Drive innovative problem-solving on challenging engineering problems, including reusable and responsive UI components, app global state management, validations and error handling, theme management, rendering performance, and JS bundle optimizations.</li>
<li><strong>Collaborate with a broad set of stakeholders from Production Management to Customer Success</strong>&nbsp;to enable well-informed business decision-making by bringing appropriate information to discussions, clarifying scope, accurately presenting tradeoffs, and communicating potential scope changes and alternatives with associated pros/cons.</li>
<li>Be an<strong>&nbsp;inspiring and supportive teammate</strong>&nbsp;by following the engineering processes, communicating often and quickly, participating in PR reviews, pair programming, active feedback, idea generation, coaching, learning, and being a delightful human being.</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>We're looking for someone who is:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><strong>A problem solver.</strong>&nbsp;You aren't afraid to go deep, experiment, learn on the fly, and do the work.</li>
<li>Passionate about turning complex business requirements into elegant software that delights users.</li>
<li><strong>Flexible.&nbsp;</strong>When given a UI/UX spec and requirements, you can achieve the resulting behavior, look, and feel, and help create the associated APIs to drive the backend data.</li>
<li>Experienced as a meaningful contributor to a data-driven architecture at scale.</li>
<li><strong>Humble but opinionated.&nbsp;</strong>You have lots of ideas, yet you are happy to shift directions when a better path emerges. You take your work much more seriously than you take yourself.</li>
<li><strong>An active collaborator.&nbsp;</strong>You want feedback on your work early and often, and you enjoy a collaborative process because it challenges you to do better work.</li>
<li>Hungry for meaningful work and space to do it. Knack is a complex product in a complex space, and the work is extremely challenging - but also deeply rewarding. Knack has a significant impact on the operations of thousands of companies and organizations.</li>
<li>Comfortable in dynamic environments, making measurable progress amid competing priorities.</li>
<li>Comfortable working both independently and as part of a&nbsp;<strong>collaborative team environment.</strong></li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Qualifications:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><strong>4+ years&nbsp;</strong>of experience shipping enterprise-grade products.</li>
<li>4+ years of extensive experience and proficiency in JavaScript, TypeScript,&nbsp;<strong>React</strong>, and CSS.</li>
<li>Experience building highly&nbsp;<strong>reusable components</strong>&nbsp;and writing unit/integration tests.</li>
<li>Experience with API design and development.</li>
<li>Experience with modern software engineering development and automation tools like Git and CI/CD pipelines.</li>
<li>Excellent interpersonal and&nbsp;<strong>communication skills</strong>, both written and verbal.</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Bonus points:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Experience with AI technologies (CoPilot/Cursor, MCP server, agentic platform, etc.)</li>
<li>Experience with Node.js, Tailwind, Tanstack, Zod, and Vue</li>
<li>Experience with custom design systems and accessibility</li>
<li>Experience in the no-code/low-code platforms</li>
<li>A degree in computer science or software engineering</li>
</ul>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/knack-senior-frontend-engineer-react-next-js-full-time-contract">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/knack-senior-frontend-engineer-react-next-js-full-time-contract</a></p>

<p>

<h3>CRO Design Specialist Job Spec&nbsp;</h3><h3>Releaf exists to make medicinal cannabis accessible, supportive, and stigma-free. Every touchpoint we create — including our digital experiences — should reflect care, clarity, and compassion.&nbsp;</h3><h3><strong>Our CRO Specialist plays a key role in shaping the way people interact with our services online, ensuring our pages are not only visually strong but also intuitive and optimised for impact.</strong></h3><h3><strong>Who we’re looking for</strong></h3><h3><strong>The CRO Design Specialist is responsible for designing user-focused web pages / landing pages and running structured testing that improves conversion, user experience, psychology and accessibility. This role will focus on landing pages, particularly from paid channels in order to improve conversions and drive ROAS.</strong></h3><p><strong>You’ll have a background in landing page design,Google analytics, Experiment software, A/B testing and user experience (UX) design or research, marketing and minimal HTML knowledge. Ideally from a fast-paced medium-sized organisation with evidence of previous suggestions and changes you have made, along with the ROI/ROAS gains achieved.</strong></p><p></p><p><strong>You’ll have solid experience in designing landing pages and digital user journeys with a strong proficiency in Figma and/or Adobe Creative Suite/design software. Not only this but you’ll have hands-on experience running CRO tests, testing hypothesis and interpreting results with an understanding of UX, responsive design, and accessibility best practices.<br><br></strong></p><p><strong>We’re looking for someone who has strong design/UX experience but also HTML/CSS editing experience, is Familiar with GA4, Tag Manager, and CRO tools like VWO/Optimizely. Design experience is essential, along with an understanding of analytic tools such as GA4 and Posthog.</strong></p><p></p><p><strong>Role Responsibilities&nbsp;&nbsp;</strong></p><h3><strong>Page &amp; Landing Page Design</strong></h3><ul><li><h3><strong>Design clean, engaging and conversion-led landing pages for campaigns and product journeys.</strong></h3></li><li><h3><strong>Produce wireframes, prototypes, and visual assets using Figma or equivalent tools.</strong></h3></li><li><h3><strong>Ensure pages follow Releaf’s brand identity, tone, and accessibility standards</strong></h3></li><li><h3><strong>Collaborate closely with the Paid Media Specialist and Perfoemance Marketing team to align design outputs.<br><br></strong></h3></li></ul><h3><strong>CRO&nbsp; Testing</strong></h3><ul><li><h3><strong>Hypothesise, plan, implement, and analyse A/B and multivariate tests via platforms such as VWO, Optimizely, or Convert.</strong></h3></li><li><h3><strong>Build variants using software,low-code editors or basic HTML/CSS where needed.</strong></h3></li><li><h3><strong>Create clear hypotheses, test plans, and measurable success criteria.</strong></h3></li><li><h3><strong>Conduct thorough pre-launch and post-launch QA to ensure stability, reliability and data accuracy.</strong></h3></li><li><h3><strong>Test entire patient journey from a patient perspectives<br><br></strong></h3></li></ul><h3><strong>Insight &amp; Behaviour Understanding</strong></h3><ul><li><h3><strong>Use GA4, Hotjar/Clarity, or similar tools to analyse user behaviour and highlight conversion barriers.</strong></h3></li><li><h3><strong>Conduct funnel analysis and produce actionable insights that influence new page designs.</strong></h3></li><li><h3><strong>Evaluate test results and provide clear, narrative-driven reporting to stakeholders.</strong></h3></li></ul><h3>Cross-Team Collaboration</h3><ul><li><h3><strong>Partner with paid specialist, performance marketing and wider organisation to deliver high-impact digital experiences.</strong></h3></li><li><h3><strong>Present findings and recommendations in a way that is accessible and easy to act on.</strong></h3></li></ul><p></p><ul><li><strong>Champion a test-and-learn culture within the performance marketing function.</strong></li></ul><li></li><p><strong>We’re looking for someone who is excited to join a busy growing organisation - our team culture is based on support and respect and we take a proactive approach to employee progression and development. The ideal candidate for this role will be happy to roll their sleeves up and can take ownership of this role. In return, we offer flexible working; personalised training and development; private health insurance, Employee Assistance Programmes; access to employee perks and Releaf staff patient benefits.&nbsp;</strong></p><p></p><p></p>
