If you love solving operational problems, building clean systems, and partnering directly with clients, you’ll thrive here.</p><p></p><h3>Position Responsibilities</h3><ul><li><p><strong>Lead and manage </strong><a href="http://monday.com">monday.com</a><strong> implementations</strong> from Discovery through Delivery across multiple clients simultaneously.</p></li><li><p>Facilitate discovery sessions to understand client goals, current-state processes, pain points, and success metrics.</p></li><li><p>Design and implement solutions leveraging <a href="http://monday.com">monday.com</a><strong> best practices</strong>, including advanced workflows, board architecture, permissions, and automations.</p></li><li><p>Build and configure within the <a href="http://monday.com">monday.com</a><strong> CRM product</strong>, including pipelines, lead/customer tracking, intake processes, and reporting dashboards.</p></li><li><p>Create and manage project plans, timelines, documentation, and communications to keep stakeholders aligned and delivery on track.</p></li><li><p>Deliver client-facing trainings, workshops, and enablement sessions that drive adoption and confident day-to-day usage.</p></li><li><p>Troubleshoot platform, workflow, and configuration issues; provide clear recommendations and iterate quickly.</p></li><li><p>Partner cross-functionally with internal teammates to ensure consistent quality, reusable assets, and scalable delivery practices.</p></li><li><p>Stay up-to-date on <a href="http://monday.com">monday.com</a> releases and proactively recommend improvements to clients and internal standards.</p><p></p></li></ul><h3>Preferred Qualifications</h3><p><em>(If you don’t meet every item but you’re excited about the role, we still encourage you to apply.)</em></p><ul><li><p><strong>2+ years experience</strong> implementing <a href="http://monday.com">monday.com</a> for clients or internal stakeholders, with clear ownership of delivery outcomes.</p></li><li><p>Demonstrated experience with the <a href="http://monday.com">monday.com</a><strong> CRM product</strong> (pipelines, contact/account structures, lead management, dashboards, reporting).</p></li><li><p>Proven ability to <strong>successfully lead multiple concurrent client projects</strong>, including managing scope, priorities, and stakeholder expectations.</p></li><li><p>Strong process mapping and solution-design skills (translating messy processes into clean, usable workflows).</p></li><li><p>Experience delivering trainings, facilitating workshops, and communicating technical concepts in a clear, client-friendly way.</p></li><li><p><a href="http://monday.com">monday.com</a> certifications are a plus (not required).</p><p></p></li></ul><h3>What We’re Looking For</h3><p>Someone who is:</p><ul><li><p>A confident consultant who can <strong>own delivery and drive outcomes</strong></p></li><li><p>Comfortable leading discovery, making recommendations, and advising clients</p></li><li><p>Organized and proactive with strong project management instincts</p></li><li><p>CRM Product experience. we provide ongoing support and training to ensure seamless adoption of new technologies.</p><p>We are an award-winning company with a multitude of notable partnerships and achievements, including:</p><ul><li><p><a href="http://monday.com">monday.com</a> Professional Services Partner of the Year 2023</p></li><li><p><a href="http://monday.com">monday.com</a> North American Partner of the Year 2021</p><p></p></li></ul><h3>About the Benefits &amp;       <title>Level: Senior Frontend Engineer</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state>North Carolina</state>
      <skills>GraphQL, JavaScript, React, UI/UX, Front-End Dev, TypeScript, Websockets, Tailwind, and AI Chatbots</skills>
      <category>Front-End Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description><img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0016/4046/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" />

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Asheville, NC
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://level.io">https://level.io</a>
</p>

<div>Level is a modern RMM platform built around real-time data, automation, and an uncompromising focus on UX. We're profitable, growing, and shipping fast with a small team. Our platform manages devices across thousands of organizations. We're hiring because there's more to build than one frontend developer can handle.<br><br>You'll be the second UI developer on the product. The surface area is large, the problems are complex, and the pace is fast. You'll work on peer-to-peer remote control, a powerful automation engine, and an AI-powered virtual technician we're bringing to market this year. We need someone who can carry that weight, not someone looking for a place to settle in.<br><br><strong>What you'll work on<br></strong><br>Level isn't a CRUD app.</div>
<ul>
<li>Scale — Our platform manages a distributed network of user devices. You'll build interfaces that surface real-time data across that network reliably and fast.</li>
<li>Real-time remote control — WebRTC peer-to-peer connections that let users manage devices with low latency across unpredictable networks.</li>
<li>Automation — A visual engine that lets IT teams build complex, multi-step workflows. The kind of UI that's easy to use and hard to build.</li>
<li>AI virtual technician — A new feature we're building this year. You'll help shape and ship the interface from the ground up.</li>
<li>Everything else — This is a small company. You'll touch dashboards, data visualization, forms, real-time updates, and things we haven't thought of yet.</li>
</ul>
<div><br><strong>Who you are</strong></div>
<ul>
<li>You have deep, senior-level JavaScript and frontend experience.&nbsp;</li>
<li>You use AI tools extensively — Codex, Claude, or whatever works — and you've spent real time figuring out how to get the most out of them. You also know when the output is wrong and can fix it. AI makes experienced developers faster. It doesn't replace the experience.</li>
<li>You're a builder. You want to shape the product, not just execute a list of tasks someone else defined.</li>
<li>You don't need to be managed. You see what needs to happen and you make it happen.</li>
<li>You've worked with TypeScript, React, Next.js, and GraphQL. If you haven't used all of them, you're the kind of person who picks things up fast because you understand the fundamentals.</li>
</ul>
<div><br><strong>Why you might want this job</strong></div>
<ul>
<li>The problems are genuinely hard — real-time systems, peer-to-peer connections, complex UIs on top of a large, distributed device network.</li>
<li>You'll have enormous ownership. There are no layers between you and the product.</li>
<li>We're profitable and growing. This isn't a company burning through runway hoping for a miracle.</li>
<li>Small team, no bureaucracy. When you ship something, it's in front of customers immediately.</li>
</ul>
<div><br><strong>Why you might not</strong></div>
<ul>
<li>We're a small team. There's no one to hand things off to and no one to share the load with when it's heavy.</li>
<li>We move fast and expect you to keep up. If you're used to spending months on a single feature, this will feel different.</li>
<li>You'll own the entire frontend surface area alongside one other developer. If you want to specialize in one corner, this isn't the role.</li>
<li>You'll context-switch. A lot. You might fix a gnarly WebRTC bug in the morning and build a new dashboard view in the afternoon.</li>
<li>We rely heavily on async communication. You need to write clearly and work effectively without waiting to be told what's next.</li>
</ul>
<div><br><strong>Details</strong></div>
<ul>
<li>Compensation: $180,000 USD</li>
<li>Location: Fully remote. Level is based in the US. International candidates are welcome — we require at least 5 hours of overlap with US Eastern time (9am–5pm ET).</li>
<li>Benefits: Medical, dental, and vision insurance (US employees). Hardware, software, and books — whatever you need to do your job.</li>
</ul>
<div><br><strong>How to apply<br></strong></div>
<div>&nbsp;</div>
<div>Apply at <a href="https://tally.so/r/ODAKrA">https://tally.so/r/ODAKrA</a></div>
<div><br>Tell us why you're interested in Level and this role specifically. We value clear, thoughtful communication.</div>
<div>&nbsp;</div>
<div>For questions reach out to hiring@level.io.</div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/level-senior-frontend-engineer">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/level-senior-frontend-engineer</a></p>
</description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Florida
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="http://www.speechify.com">http://www.speechify.com</a>
</p>

<p><strong>Overview</strong></p>
<p>With the growth of our iOS app, being the #18 productivity app in the App Store category and also our recent recognition as Apple's 2025 Design Award for inclusivity, we find the need for a Senior iOS Engineer to help us support the new user base as well as work on new and exciting projects to push our missing forward. &nbsp;&nbsp;</p>
<p>This is a key role and ideal for someone who thinks strategically, enjoys fast-paced environments, passionate about making product decisions, and has experience building great user experiences that delight users.</p>
<p>We are a flat organization that allows anyone to become a leader by showing excellent technical skills and delivering results consistently and fast. Work ethic, solid communication skills, and obsession with winning are paramount.&nbsp;</p>
<p>Our interview process involves several technical interviews and we aim to complete them within 1 week.&nbsp;</p>
<p><strong>What Yo</strong><strong>u’</strong><strong>ll Do</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Opportunity to lead key engineering and product decisions</li>
<li>Actively shipping production code for the Speechify iOS app</li>
<li>Work within a dedicated product team</li>
<li>Participate in product discussions to shape the product roadmap</li>
<li>Maintain and enhance the existing complex app architecture</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>An Ideal Candidate Should Have</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Experience. You've worked on products that scaled to a large user base</li>
<li>Track record. You have worked on various products from inception to decent traction. You have been responsible for engineering the product</li>
<li>Customer obsession. We expect every team member whose responsibilities directly impact customers to be constantly obsessed about providing the best possible experience</li>
<li>Product thinking. You make thoughtful decisions about the evolution of your product and support internal teams and designers into taking the right direction</li>
<li>Speed. You work quickly to generate ideas and know how to decide which things can ship now and what things need time</li>
<li>Focus. We’re a high-growth startup with a busy, remote team. You know how and when to engage or be heads down</li>
<li>Technical skills. Swift, SwiftUI</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Technical Requirements:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Swift Programming Language</li>
<li>SwiftUI experience</li>
<li>Experience in Multithreading Programming</li>
<li>Working with CI/CD infrastructure</li>
<li>Experience with Fastlane</li>
<li>SOLID principles, the ability to write every single class according to SOLID</li>
<li>Experience with Git and understanding of different Git strategies</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>What We offer:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>A fast-growing environment where you can help shape the company and product</li>
<li>An entrepreneurial crew that supports risk, intuition, and hustle</li>
<li>The opportunity to make a big impact in a transformative industry</li>
<li>A competitive salary, a collegiate atmosphere, and a commitment to building a great asynchronous culture</li>
<li>Work on a product that millions of people use and where daily feedback includes users sharing that they cried when they first found the product because it was so impactful on their lives</li>
<li>Support people with learning differences like Dyslexia, ADD, Low Vision, Concussions, Autism, and Second Language Learners, and give reading superpowers to professionals all over the world</li>
<li>Work in one of the fastest growing sectors of tech: Intersection of Artificial Intelligence and Audio</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Think you’re a good fit for this job?&nbsp;</strong></p>
<p>Tell us more about yourself and why you're interested in the role when you apply.<br>And don’t forget to include links to your portfolio and LinkedIn.</p>
<p><strong>Not looking but know someone who would make a great fit?&nbsp;</strong></p>
<p>Refer them!&nbsp;</p>
<p><strong>Speechify is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace.&nbsp;</strong></p>
<p>Speechify does not discriminate on the basis of race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, or other legally protected status.</p>

      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country>🇺🇸 United States of America</country>
      <state>Maryland</state>
      <skills>Figma, HTML/CSS, JavaScript, and UI/UX</skills>
      <category>Front-End Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description><img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0170/9402/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" />

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> New York, NY
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://mobena.co/">https://mobena.co/</a>
</p>

<p class="MsoNormal">Mobena is a three-person, remote-first startup building the “evolution” of multi-generational tax/estate planning software, targeting the advisors of America’s 2 million wealthiest families. We’re currently hiring our first dedicated UI Engineer to “own” the frontend of our flagship product, Mobena Evolution, for which we have little to no frontend code. You’ll work directly with our backend/algorithm-focused CTO, as well as our designer-founder, to turn evolving Figma concepts into fast, maintainable, and scalable user interfaces.</p>
<p class="MsoNormal"><strong>What You’ll Do</strong></p>
<ul>
<li class="MsoListParagraph">Choose and justify the frameworks and tools that best fit our needs.</li>
<ul>
<li class="MsoListParagraph">We have some holdover prototype code in Vite JS, but you can take it or leave it; all of our Figma concepts are new.</li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph">The backend is a standard HTTP API. We are open to everything from established web stacks (React, Angular, Svelte, Vite) to cross-platform or native solutions (Qt, WASM, .NET MAUI, Tauri, Electron, Tk)—literally anything, if you can convince us it’s the right choice.</li>
</ul>
<li class="MsoListParagraph">Build our UI from the ground up, establishing clear, maintainable patterns for components, state management, styling, testing, performance, and accessibility.</li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph">Collaborate to refine and sometimes re-frame existing screen designs in Figma.</li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph">Connect the frontend to our APIs (REST, GraphQL, or similar).</li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph">Lay groundwork for future mobile clients; however, experience in this regard is a plus rather than a requirement.</li>
</ul>
<p class="MsoNormal"><strong>About You</strong></p>
<ul>
<li class="MsoListParagraph">You’ve shipped an interactive application—web, desktop, or mobile—via coursework, open source, side project or professional work.</li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph">You care about usability and design quality. (That is, you care about the user.)</li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph">You learn by <em>doing:</em> building things rather than sitting in committee.</li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph">You are happy to say <em>I don’t know.</em> We try to cultivate <a title="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shoshin" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shoshin">“beginner’s mind”</a> at Mobena—it helps us avoid conventional traps and groupthink. The first step of thinking like a beginner is acknowledging your own ignorance! 😊</li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph">You can defend your technical choices in clear English, and can take credit for your successes as well your mistakes.</li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph">You are comfortable in an early-stage, collaborative environment.</li>
</ul>
